_doc: K2 v2 exact decision instrument for alpha(U-world): 289 (world record) vs 288 (architecture closed). DGX /tmp/foundry_wave2/v2exact/. Round 87 endgame.
question: Does U-world contain 289 mutually-compatible lines? (132 W2 + 289 U = 421 lines = 842 pts = WR over 841). Or alpha(U)=288?
verdict: NO 289-WITNESS FOUND. alpha(U-world)=288 strongly evidenced (complete exact neighborhood proofs at levels 1-2 + broad search), but NOT globally machine-proven. No world record from this route on current evidence.

structural_reproduction_exact.note: vertex = (8-support cell H, even-sign class); conflict iff |sum_{H&H'} s_i s'_i| >= 5; |cos| in {0,1/8,1/4,3/8,1/2}

encoding_chosen.form: structural per-cell-pair sign-dot blocks (64x64 |dot|>=5), NOT raw pairwise-from-file
encoding_chosen.full_graph_status: MEMORY-INFEASIBLE on the shared 121GB DGX (constraint proto build hit ~20GB RSS with only ~8GB free -> aborted to protect the CHRONOS stack). Bounded-universe models (<=~27 cells) build+solve fine.
encoding_chosen.warm_start: 288 U-witness accepted as AddHint in every bounded run; CP-SAT never improved on it in any universe.

LEVEL1_complete.claim: MIS(P0-slice + any single foreign cell) = 288 for ALL 480 foreign cells
LEVEL1_complete.implication: PROVEN: any 289 config uses >=2 cells outside the P0-slice

LEVEL2_complete.claim: MIS(P0-slice + any 2 foreign cells) = 288 for ALL 39 B6-orbit representatives (B6 = pairing stabilizer, order 46080)
LEVEL2_complete.implication: PROVEN: any 289 config uses >=3 cells outside the P0-slice

LEVEL3_complete.claim: MIS(P0-slice + any 3 foreign cells) = 288 for ALL 1284 B6-orbit representatives of foreign triples
LEVEL3_complete.method: scipy connected_components under a 3-element B6 generating set; VALIDATED (k=2 path reproduces the 39 pair-orbits exactly)
LEVEL3_complete.implication: PROVEN: any 289 config uses >=4 cells outside the P0-slice

levels_4_5_6_sampled.note: randk.json: random samples of P0-slice + k foreign cells, ~120 universes each, all closing to (attempted) OPTIMAL; best=288 everywhere, 0 records

twoslice_unions.claim: MIS(P0-slice UNION P1-slice) for all 31 pairings P1 within one element-swap of P0
twoslice_unions.note: richer than slice+2; still capped at 288

randk_level_3_to_6.note: random samples of P0-slice + k foreign cells, k in {3,4,5,6}; see randk.json

large_universe_hunts.anchored_100cell: no 289 across 20+ diverse near-slice universes (obj=288 every iter; CP-SAT LNS never improved the warm start)
large_universe_hunts.free_100cell: random cell universes reach only ~142 (structure-poor) -> confirms slice specialness

LP_with_all_slice_cuts.note: x=0.3 uniform; each vertex in 315 of 10395 slice constraints

rigorous_but_loose_upper_bounds.conclusion: Massive integrality gap (288 vs 583/1030/9504). No tractable convex relaxation reaches 289; a proof needs exact combinatorics or a bespoke counting theorem.

why_not_proven: Full exact MIS is intractable within budget: (a) the 23.5M-constraint model OOMs the shared box; (b) CP-SAT's dual bound is very weak here (301 at 45 cells / 60s) so it cannot certify OPTIMAL beyond ~27-cell universes; (c) all convex relaxations have a huge gap. The clean proof requires extending the slice-closure theorem to CROSS-slice (odd-overlap 5/7, |cos| in {1/8,3/8}) configurations.

next_step.option_A_complete_ladder: Enumerate B6-orbits of foreign TRIPLES/QUADS and prove MIS(slice+3), MIS(slice+4)=288 for each (universes 18-21 cells, close to OPTIMAL). Pushes the proven lower bound on #foreign-cells a 289 must use; combined with a diameter/structure argument could close it.
next_step.option_B_theorem: Prove no independent set with odd-overlap (defect-intersection 1 or 3) cell pairs exceeds 288 -- the odd-eighth |cos| in {1/8,3/8} interaction is the only escape from the Hanani-capped even-overlap (A+W4) world.
next_step.runnable: ssh dgx; cd /tmp/foundry_wave2/v2exact; write level3.py (foreign-triple B6 orbits via stabilizer chain) then: /tmp/sdpvenv/bin/python level3.py --seconds 120 --workers 8
